sections.paragraph:
προγενής, ές gen. οῦς born in early times, primeval comp. προγενέστερος, α, ον older (Hom. et al.; Theodotus [II b.c.] in Eus., PE 9, 22, 3=Denis p. 205, 27; Tat. 41, 1 [comp.]) τινός than someth. of the Son of God πάσης τῆς κτίσεως αὐτοῦ προγενέστερος older than his whole creation Hs 9, 12, 2.—DELG s.v. γίγνομαι.

gen. gen. = genitive

comp. comp. = comparative(ly)

Hom Hom , VIII b.c.—List 5

et al. et al. = et alii (and others)

Theodotus Theodotus before I b.c.—List 5

Eus. Eus. = Eusebius of Caesarea, various works; IV a.d.—List 5

Denis Denis = AD., Fragmenta pseudepigraphorum quae supersunt Graeca—Lists 2 beg., 5, 6

Tat Tat , II a.d.—List 5

someth. someth. = something

Hs Hs = Similitudes

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 866
